verdict_reasoning: TGFB1 is secreted as a large latent complex (LLC) in which the LAP prodomain remains disulfide-bonded to the mature TGF-β1 dimer, and the entire complex is covalently tethered to LTBP proteins that can anchor it to the extracellular matrix or, critically, to the cell surface via covalent linkage to TM proteins such as GARP (LRRC32) and LRRC33. On regulatory T cells and other cell types, this GARP–proTGFB1 complex is stably displayed on the plasma membrane as a wash-resistant, covalently tethered entity, making the protein body surface accessible. This is a documented, cell-type-specific surface form, qualifying as contextual/stable_surface_attachment.
